title: Optimizing Writes to Hybrid Tables
author: Adam Timm
description: Optimize write performance for Hybrid Tables: plan cache mechanics, bound variables, stored procedure overhead, COPY INTO behavior, and DML anti-patterns.

plan_cache_mechanics.how_it_works: Snowflake computes query_parameterized_hash by replacing literals in predicates with parameters, then hashing canonicalized SQL text. Same hash = plan reuse.
what_shares_same_hash: Literal value differences in comparison operators (=, !=, >=, <=)
what_breaks_hash: Different function calls (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP vs literal timestamp)

performance_hierarchy: 1. Direct DML with AUTOCOMMIT=TRUE (lowest overhead, plan cache optimal)
2. Multi-statement transaction (BEGIN/COMMIT) — slight overhead, useful for batching
3. Stored procedure — highest overhead, use only when procedural logic required

copy_into_behavior.on_error: Only ABORT_STATEMENT supported (not SKIP_FILE or CONTINUE)
copy_into_behavior.optimized_path: Only on empty tables (never had data loaded). Shows 'Number of rows bulk loaded' in query profile.
copy_into_behavior.non_empty_throughput: ~1 million rows per minute (varies with row width and index count)
copy_into_behavior.ctas_vs_copy: CTAS and COPY INTO empty HT both use optimized path. CTAS does not support FOREIGN KEY.

anti_patterns.name: String concatenation instead of bound variables
anti_patterns.impact: Every unique literal combination compiles a new plan (10-100ms overhead per query)

anti_patterns.name: CTAS repeated against populated table
anti_patterns.impact: Re-creates all indexes from scratch every execution; use MERGE or incremental INSERT instead

anti_patterns.name: Single-row INSERTs in a loop
anti_patterns.impact: N round-trips for N rows; use batch APIs (executemany, addBatch, array binds)

anti_patterns.name: BI tool running GROUP BY directly on HT
anti_patterns.impact: COLUMN_BASED scan every execution; snapshot to ST first

anti_patterns.name: Inconsistent schema qualification
anti_patterns.impact: Splits plan cache into multiple entries; reduces effective hit rate

diagnostic_checklist: compilation_time > 50% of total_elapsed_time → plan cache not warming (check hash cardinality)
Multiple parameterized hashes for same logical operation → schema qualification inconsistency
CALL latency >> child INSERT latency → SP overhead, consider removing SP wrapper
hybrid_table_requests_throttled_count > 0 → too many concurrent requests hitting row store
recommended_batch_size: 500-1000 rows per batch call (profile your specific row width)
